proven.claim: PROVEN: `result.path` is a non-empty/non-null value when line 471 executes.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 471
; PROVEN: `result.path` is a non-empty/non-null value when line 471 executes.
; The enclosing `if (result.path)` guard establishes this. Negate → expect unsat.

(declare-const result_path_truthy Int)   ; 1 iff result.path is non-empty string
(declare-const line471_reached    Int)   ; 1 iff line 471 executes

; Code transition: line 471 is inside `if (result.path)` — only reached when path is truthy
(assert (= line471_reached (ite (= result_path_truthy 1) 1 0)))

; Negation of property: line 471 executes but result.path is falsy
(assert (= line471_reached    1))
(assert (= result_path_truthy 0))

(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: `result.path` is truthy (guard passed) but the code never verifies
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 471
; VIOLATION: `result.path` is truthy (guard passed) but the code never verifies
; that the file at result.path was actually written. install() may return a
; computed path even on a partial failure, so the log implies existence that
; cannot be guaranteed.

(declare-const result_path_truthy  Int)   ; 1 iff result.path is non-empty
(declare-const path_exists_on_disk Int)   ; 1 iff file at result.path exists
(declare-const line471_reached     Int)   ; 1 iff line 471 executes

; Code transition: line 471 fires because result.path is truthy (guard passed)
(assert (= line471_reached (ite (= result_path_truthy 1) 1 0)))
(assert (= line471_reached    1))
(assert (= result_path_truthy 1))

; Code transition: no existsSync / statSync call between install() and line 471
;   — path_exists_on_disk is never checked by the code

; Violation: a non-empty path is logged pointing to a file that was never written
(assert (= path_exists_on_disk 0))

(check-sat)
